title: Salty Tender Nostalgic American Fish Stir-fry
description: A pescatarian-friendly dish featuring a tender and salty fish preparation with the addition of white wine for added depth.
instructions: Season cod fillet with soy sauce, salt, and pepper on both sides.
In a large skillet or wok over medium heat, add sesame oil and sauté garlic and ginger until fragrant (about 1 minute).
Add white wine if using, and let it simmer for about 2 minutes to deglaze the pan.
Carefully place cod fillet on top of the aromatic base, skin-side down. Cook for about 5-7 minutes or until golden brown.
Flip the fish and cook for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring that both sides are golden brown.

Transfer cooked fish to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, season with salt and pepper again if needed, then bake in oven at 375°F for about 10-12 minutes until fish is thoroughly cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat scallions and bell peppers in the same skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté until tender (about 2-3 minutes).
Remove fish from oven and top each piece with a generous portion of sautéed vegetables. Garnish with additional chopped green onions if desired.
Serve hot as a main course or light dinner accompaniment.
